channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Officers who defended Capitol from rioters sue to block payouts from fund
item.description: Two police officers who helped defend the U.S. Capitol during the Jan. 6 riot are suing to block anyone from receiving payouts from a new settlement fund.
item.content:encoded:
Two police officers who helped defend the U.S. Capitol during the Jan. 6 riot are suing to block anyone from receiving payouts from a new settlement fund.

(Image credit: John Minchillo)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Ex-prosecutor charged with sending to herself report on Trump classified probe
item.description: The former prosecutor faces federal charges over allegations that she sent a report on Jack Smith's investigation into President Trump's hoarding of classified documents to her personal email account.
item.content:encoded:
The former prosecutor faces federal charges over allegations that she sent a report on Jack Smith's investigation into President Trump's hoarding of classified documents to her personal email account.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Liberal U.S. mayors team up with European counterparts to fight authoritarianism
item.description: Ten U.S. mayors from cities such as Chicago and Cincinnati have joined a pact with European mayors to defend democracy and progressive values and fight right-wing populists and authoritarianism.
item.content:encoded:
Ten U.S. mayors from cities such as Chicago and Cincinnati have joined a pact with European mayors to defend democracy and progressive values and fight right-wing populists and authoritarianism.

(Image credit: Courtesy of the city of Bratislava)
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: Senate panel hears testimony on online sports betting, prediction markets
item.description: Senate lawmakers grilled sports betting industry officials during a hearing focused on recent cheating scandals, companies' marketing tactics and regulatory battles.
item.content:encoded:
Senate lawmakers grilled sports betting industry officials during a hearing focused on recent cheating scandals, companies' marketing tactics and regulatory battles.

(Image credit: Mariam Zuhaib)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: Advice for 2026 commencement speakers: Don't bring up AI
item.description: Commencement speakers who bring up the sweeping changes that artificial intelligence is driving are facing boos from the Class of 2026.
item.content:encoded:
Commencement speakers who bring up the sweeping changes that artificial intelligence is driving are facing boos from the Class of 2026.

(Image credit: University of Central Florida and Middle Tennessee State University via Storyful)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: Montana could soon ease restrictions on mining precious metals
item.description: Montana's 30-year-old restrictions on mining for precious metals like gold could soon be relaxed, as the mining industry makes a comeback in the U.S.
item.content:encoded: Montana's 30-year-old restrictions on mining for precious metals like gold could soon be relaxed, as the mining industry makes a comeback in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ellis Juhlin

item.title: In Kansas City, World Cup preparations include a new transit system
item.description: FIFA host cities are gearing up to transport thousands of fans to stadiums for the soccer matches. Kansas city is going all out on adding new buses and routes.
item.content:encoded: FIFA host cities are gearing up to transport thousands of fans to stadiums for the soccer matches. Kansas city is going all out on adding new buses and routes.
item.dc:creator: Savannah Hawley-Bates

item.title: After the Kars4Kids ad is banned in California, we check in on nostalgic jingles past
item.description: A California judge has given Kars4Kids 30 days to either take its ads off the air or update them to disclose its affiliations to a Jewish charity based in the Northeast.
item.content:encoded: A California judge has given Kars4Kids 30 days to either take its ads off the air or update them to disclose its affiliations to a Jewish charity based in the Northeast.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: U.S. grand jury indicts Raúl Castro, former Cuban president
item.description: The 94-year-old former leader of Cuba faces several charges, including four counts of murder for an attack on a humanitarian group more than 30 years ago.
item.content:encoded:
The 94-year-old former leader of Cuba faces several charges, including four counts of murder for an attack on a humanitarian group more than 30 years ago.

(Image credit: Alexandre Meneghini)
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: The Justice Department gives Trump an unprecedented settlement
item.description: D.O.J. gave Trump and his family immunity from tax audits and set up a $1.8 billion fund for victims of "government weaponization." Former government lawyer Andrew Weissmann explains the settlement.
item.content:encoded: D.O.J. gave Trump and his family immunity from tax audits and set up a $1.8 billion fund for victims of "government weaponization." Former government lawyer Andrew Weissmann explains the settlement.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross
